headline: Trump and Iran's president sign framework agreement at Château de Versailles
description: The memorandum of understanding, which the US president said he 'just signed' as he exited the Château de Versailles after a G7 summit, had earlier been slated for signatures by Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and US Vice President JD Vance. Iran said an in-person ceremony was no longer needed.

headline: Tebnine hospital bears witness to ongoing war in southern Lebanon
On Donbas front, warehouse manager aims to improve daily life for Ukrainian soldiers
Trump's shift on Ukraine welcomed by G7 leaders
description: The American president indicated a tougher line against Moscow during the three-day meeting in France, which was hailed as a success by G7 leaders. After the summit, he accompanied French President Emmanuel Macron on a private tour of Versailles, followed by a candlelit dinner with 30 guests.

headline: Insee lowers growth forecasts as French households bear brunt of Middle East crisis
description: The French economy is expected to move at a sluggish pace through 2026, with growth reaching just 0.7% by year's end instead of the previously anticipated 0.9%.

headline: MMA in Le Monde: From clandestine beginnings in France to the spotlight in front of the White House
description: Mixed martial arts has just experienced unprecedented media exposure thanks to US President Trump. In France, this combat sport, whose name first appeared in 2006 in the newspaper, had to fight to earn respect.

headline: The French villages left behind by the long-abandoned hovertrain project
description: In the Petite Beauce region, a derelict monorail runs for 18 km through farmland, fueling nostalgia for a bygone era of industrial splendor.

headline: 2026 World Cup: Cristiano Ronaldo, Luka Modric and Edin Dzeko, still in the game in their 40s

Weakened at home, Macron counts on 'his' last G7 to boost his diplomatic record
description: As his second term grows increasingly complex at home amid the killing of 11-year-old Lyhanna, the French president, who is chairing the G7 summit in Evian, is staking his legitimacy on an ambitious international agenda.

headline: French environment minister's absence from public debate underscores government's climate inaction
description: Appiointed in October 2025, Monique Barbut is struggling to make her mark. This situation goes beyond her as an individual and highlights how the government has abandoned the issue.

headline: From insider trading in France to an American cell phone: How Spain tracked down former prime minister Zapatero
description: Judicial cooperation among several countries, including France and Switzerland, has helped uncover an international network relying on 'opaque financial channels,' which were allegedly used, among other things, to pay the former Socialist leader.
